note: The FUNCTIONAL axis of a body, orthogonal to body_type (the structural/constitutional-form axis). A body carries zero or more functions; the same function cross-cuts several body_types (e.g. regulators are non_ministerial_department, executive_ndpb, public_corporation or other_body). Added in the Spiral 1.5 bolt-on so groupings the constitutional-form axis cannot express (chiefly 'regulator') are queryable and filterable on the map, WITHOUT reopening decision #12 (regulation is a function, not a body_type). Every populated tag is cited via function_source_ids (no tacit tagging). In Spiral 2 these tags become derivable from the powers/duties a body holds. Populated this pass: regulation (from an official list). Others are defined but not yet populated; fill in later sourced passes, never from memory. v0.2 (2026-07-20): adds `judicial`, distinct from `tribunal_justice` because the project already treats courts as constitutionally distinct from tribunals (body_type decision, 2026-07-06). This is the axis a map colour or query should key on for the judicial layer - NOT power_type, which distinguishes asking a court from being one.

definitions.regulation: Sets, licenses, supervises or enforces rules on a sector or profession under statutory authority.
definitions.inspection: Independently inspects/rates the performance or safety of services or premises.
definitions.advisory: Provides expert advice to ministers or Parliament; no executive power (usually body_type advisory_ndpb).
definitions.funding: Distributes public money as grants or funding awards (funding council, grant-maker).
definitions.research: Conducts or commissions research as its primary purpose (research council/institute).
definitions.cultural_heritage: Holds, curates or protects national collections, heritage or the arts (museums, libraries, galleries).
definitions.ombudsman_redress: Investigates complaints and provides redress against public bodies or a sector.
definitions.tribunal_justice: A statutory tribunal determining disputes outside the ordinary courts.
definitions.operational_delivery: Directly delivers a public service or operational function to the public.
definitions.standards_accreditation: Sets technical standards, accredits or certifies (distinct from statutory regulation).
definitions.judicial: A court exercising judicial power: determining matters between parties, or between a party and the state, and making binding orders. Distinct from `tribunal_justice` (statutory tribunals), which the project holds separately because courts and tribunals are constitutionally distinct. Tag only from a founding or constituting statute, never from tacit knowledge.
